context.story_engine: An advertising executive maintains a false identity in 1960s New York, testing how far he'll go to escape his past

plotlines.name: Don: Identity
plotlines.goal: maintain his false identity as Don Draper while hiding his past as Dick Whitman
plotlines.obstacle: Adam's reappearance, Pete's blackmail attempt, and the constant threat of exposure
plotlines.stakes: loss of career, family, and entire constructed life

plotlines.name: Don: Marriage
plotlines.goal: maintain his marriage while pursuing affairs
plotlines.obstacle: Betty's growing suspicions, his emotional distance, and his inability to be honest
plotlines.stakes: family breakdown and loss of his suburban facade

plotlines.name: Betty: Anxiety
plotlines.goal: understand and overcome her mysterious physical symptoms and emotional emptiness
plotlines.obstacle: 1960s limitations on women's autonomy, Don's deception about her therapy, societal expectations
plotlines.stakes: mental breakdown and loss of identity

plotlines.name: Pete: Advancement
plotlines.goal: gain recognition and promotion at Sterling Cooper
plotlines.obstacle: his privileged background working against him, Don's authority, his own immaturity
plotlines.stakes: career stagnation and loss of respect

plotlines.name: Peggy: Ambition
plotlines.goal: advance from secretary to copywriter in a male-dominated industry
plotlines.obstacle: gender discrimination, lack of experience, pregnancy complications
plotlines.stakes: remaining trapped in a subordinate role

plotlines.name: Roger: Affair
plotlines.goal: maintain his relationship with Joan while preserving his marriage
plotlines.obstacle: Joan's resistance to being a kept woman, his heart attacks, family obligations

plotlines.name: Don: Love
plotlines.goal: find genuine emotional connection with Rachel Menken
plotlines.obstacle: his inability to be honest about his identity, his marriage, his emotional walls

plotlines.name: Adam: Survival
plotlines.goal: reconnect with his brother Dick/Don and rebuild their relationship
plotlines.obstacle: Don's complete rejection and denial of their shared past

events.event: Don interviews an African-American waiter about smoking Old Gold cigarettes
Don reviews research showing cigarettes cause cancer with no viable Lucky Strike pitch
Don visits Midge and tells her he's stuck on the campaign
Midge suggests Don change the conversation but he leaves without a solution
Peggy arrives for her first day as Don's secretary
Joan gives Peggy a tour and advises her to improve her appearance and get birth control
Pete flirts aggressively with Peggy, belittling her in front of Don
Roger pressures Don about the Lucky Strike pitch worth twenty-five million dollars
Don meets Rachel Menken and dismisses her ambitions, pitching coupons
Rachel calls Don's approach lazy and beneath her brand
Don walks out telling Roger he won't let a woman talk to him that way
Roger orders Don to apologize to Rachel
Pete's colleagues take him to a strip club for his bachelor party
Pete tries to grope a dancer and is rebuffed
Don apologizes to Rachel over drinks and they share a candid conversation about love
Don tells Rachel that love doesn't exist, was invented to sell nylons
Pete shows up drunk at Peggy's apartment and they sleep together
Lucky Strike team arrives and Don's research presentation angers the clients
Don asks about Lucky Strike's tobacco processing and learns it's toasted
Don pitches 'It's Toasted' tagline and saves the account
Don drives to suburban house and climbs into bed with Betty, revealing his marriage

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of performance and deception — Don's false identity, hidden marriage, cynical view of love; Pete's entitled pursuit; Peggy's navigation of office dynamics
the final reveal reframes everything we've seen about Don
Pete and Peggy's storylines intersect when they sleep together

events.event: Don deflects Roger's questions about his past at dinner, saying he was raised to consider talking about yourself a sin of pride
Betty's hands go numb in the restaurant bathroom and she cannot grip her lipstick
Mona helps Betty apply lipstick, noticing her distress
Cooper and Roger tell Don the agency must help Nixon's campaign
Don rejects Paul's space-age Right Guard concept, arguing people fear the future
Paul takes Peggy to lunch and mentions women can be copywriters
Paul asks Peggy out but she declines, implying she's seeing Pete
Joan warns Peggy that her work is slipping and men treat secretaries as disposable
Betty loses control of the car due to hand numbness and crashes into a neighbor's birdbath
Betty worries that Sally's bruised face could ruin her daughter's future
Don takes Betty to see psychiatrist Dr. Wayne
Betty awkwardly describes her anxiety to Dr. Wayne, circling around her mother's death
Dr. Wayne calls Don to report on Betty's session without her knowledge
Don listens to the psychiatrist's report without telling Betty

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters maintaining facades while struggling with hidden truths or desires
Betty's psychological crisis becomes a shared marital problem when Don arranges psychiatric treatment
the audience knows Don is receiving reports about Betty's therapy sessions while she believes in doctor-patient confidentiality

events.event: A man on the commuter train recognizes Don as Dick Whitman from the Korean War
Don makes noncommittal plans with the man while revealing nothing about his current life
Pete returns from honeymoon to find his office filled with people in Chinese peasant costumes and live chickens
Pete is humiliated by the prank but forced to laugh it off
Don dismisses the Volkswagen Lemon ad while Pete argues it's brilliant
Rachel returns to Sterling Cooper for a follow-up meeting about her store
Don and Rachel's mutual attraction becomes obvious during the meeting
Rachel takes Don to the roof and shows him the guard dogs
Rachel confides about talking to dogs after her mother's death
Don and Rachel share a moment of connection over their losses
Don assembles Sally's playhouse while drinking beers in the morning
Betty reluctantly invites divorced neighbor Helen Bishop to Sally's party
The suburban wives gossip about Helen and treat her with hostility
Don has a quiet conversation with Helen while watching the children
Betty watches from the kitchen window and feels jealous
Betty sends Don to pick up the birthday cake
Don drives past the party without stopping and parks alone by railroad tracks
Don sits alone in his car drinking for an hour
Betty grows increasingly anxious about Don's absence at the party
Helen offers her Sara Lee cake as a substitute
Don returns home with a golden retriever puppy instead of the cake
Betty is furious but says nothing in front of the children

episodes.theme: the masks we wear and the isolation they create

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different forms of performance and concealment — Don hiding his past, the marriage facade, Betty's suppressed emotions
Rachel opens up about her past while Don remains guarded about his own trauma, though both share the experience of loss
Betty's jealousy and anxiety about Helen Bishop directly impacts the marriage storyline and Don's behavior

events.event: Pete and Trudy visit an expensive Park Avenue apartment that costs thirty-two thousand dollars, far beyond Pete's seventy-five-dollar salary
Pete asks his wealthy parents for financial help but his father dismisses advertising as undignified and refuses, leaving Pete humiliated
Don, Pete, and Sal prepare a pitch for Bethlehem Steel that goes poorly, with Don blaming Pete for failing to manage client expectations
Pete arranges an unauthorized meeting with Bethlehem Steel executives, bringing women as his 'cousins' and pitching his own tagline 'the backbone of America'
Bethlehem Steel calls praising Pete's idea, making Don furious who confronts Pete privately and fires him on the spot
Pete breaks down and gets drunk in his office after being fired
Bert Cooper overrules Don's firing because Pete's mother is a wealthy Dyckman, and the agency cannot afford to lose those social connections
Don and Roger tell Pete he's rehired, framing it as Don fighting to save Pete's job while hiding the real reason
Pete and Trudy have dinner with her parents, where despite Pete asking her not to mention it, Trudy asks her father for apartment money
Tom Vogel immediately agrees to provide the money, leaving Pete ashamed that his in-laws stepped in while his own family refused
Betty begins a cautious friendship with divorced neighbor Helen Bishop
Helen asks Betty to babysit her children including Glen while she volunteers at Kennedy campaign headquarters
Glen deliberately walks in on Betty in the bathroom, she scolds him sharply then softens and comforts him when he becomes frightened
Glen asks Betty for a lock of her hair and she hesitates then cuts a small lock and gives it to him
Don stops at Midge's apartment where she tells him she wants more of his time, but Don deflects and leaves

interactions.description: all three storylines explore what characters sacrifice to belong — Pete sacrifices dignity for social status, Betty transgresses boundaries for connection, Don avoids intimacy to maintain his facade
Pete believes Don saved his job out of loyalty, unaware it was purely about his family's social connections

events.event: Don wins an award for Grin-and-Barrett account, his photo appears in Advertising Age
Adam recognizes Don's photo as his half-brother Dick Whitman
Adam confronts Don in Sterling Cooper lobby, calling him Dick
Don denies knowing Adam and walks away
Adam sends Don a letter with hotel room number and childhood photograph
Don burns the childhood photograph
Don calls Adam and arranges to meet at the hotel
Don acknowledges he is Dick Whitman but declares Dick is dead
Don gives Adam $5,000 and orders him to leave New York forever
Adam takes the money, heartbroken
Ken publishes story in The Atlantic Monthly, news spreads through office
Pete reads his talking bear story aloud to Trudy at home
Pete pressures Trudy to contact ex-fiancé Charlie for publishing help
Charlie shows romantic interest in Trudy, hints at affair
Trudy refuses Charlie's advance
Trudy tells Pete Charlie offered Boy's Life, Pete is ungrateful
Trudy confronts Pete for putting her in that position
Peggy overhears Don's sexually explicit call with Midge
Betty arrives for family portrait but Don is absent meeting Adam
Peggy panics and turns to Joan for help covering Don's absence
Joan smoothly stalls Betty
Joan reprimands Peggy about keeping the boss's secrets

episodes.theme: the price of ambition and the burden of secrets

interactions.description: all three storylines explore what people sacrifice for their goals — Don sacrifices family connection, Pete sacrifices his wife's dignity, Peggy learns to sacrifice moral comfort
Peggy witnesses Don's affair call while covering for his secret meeting about his identity — she's protecting secrets she doesn't fully understand
Don's identity crisis creates the crisis Peggy must handle, which threatens his marriage stability

events.event: Don trips and falls down the stairs carrying Betty's Mother's Day breakfast tray
Don's fall triggers a flashback to young Dick Whitman watching his stepmother give birth to Adam
Sterling Cooper receives a pitch opportunity from the Israeli Tourism Board
Don contacts Rachel Menken for her perspective as a Jewish American on the Israel account
Rachel is irritated at being treated as a token Jewish consultant but agrees to meet
Rachel explains the Jewish concept of exile and Israel as a homeland after displacement
Don is visibly moved and their professional conversation shifts to personal attraction
Peggy participates in the Belle Jolie lipstick focus group and refuses to fawn over products
Peggy calls the blotted tissues 'a basket of kisses' showing her copy instinct
Freddy asks Peggy to write advertising material for Belle Jolie account
Roger suggests Joan get her own apartment to make their affair easier
Joan refuses Roger's suggestion, recognizing she would gain nothing permanent
Roger gives Joan a caged bird as a gift
Don visits Midge but finds her boyfriend Roy already there
Roy needles Don about his suburban life and the emptiness of advertising
Don and Roy spar over values at The Gaslight Cafe

interactions.description: all storylines explore displacement and the search for authentic connection — Don's false identity, Rachel's Jewish exile, Joan's transactional relationship, Don's hollow marriage, Peggy's outsider status
Rachel's explanation of exile resonates with Don's own displacement from his true identity as Dick Whitman
the folk song 'By the rivers of Babylon' creates a musical bridge connecting all characters' experiences of displacement

patches.reason: Israeli Tourism Board pitch creates its own narrative arc but doesn't fit existing storylines

events.event: Roger's wife and daughter leave town, Joan has other plans
Roger invites himself to dinner at the Draper home
Betty prepares an elaborate meal for Roger's visit
Roger drinks heavily and flirts with Betty over dinner
Roger grabs Betty's waist and nuzzles her when Don leaves
Betty pushes Roger away and he leaves quickly
Don senses something wrong and demands explanation from Betty
Betty tells Don what Roger did
Don plans elaborate humiliation instead of confronting Roger directly
Don pushes Roger to consume large quantities of food and alcohol at lunch
Don bribes Hollis to claim elevator is out of service
Roger is forced to walk up twenty-three flights of stairs
Roger vomits in front of Nixon campaign representatives
Don observes Roger's humiliation with quiet satisfaction
Pete tries to return duplicate Chip 'n' Dip but gets only store credit
Pete uses store credit to buy a .22-caliber rifle
Pete shows rifle to colleagues Paul, Ken, and Harry
Pete describes hunting fantasy to Peggy about shooting deer and having woman cook it
Trudy is angry Pete exchanged her aunt's gift for a 'stupid toy'
Betty encounters Helen Bishop at the supermarket
Helen confronts Betty about giving hair to her son Glen
Betty slaps Helen across the face in public
Peggy continues working on Belle Jolie lipstick copy
Pete learns Peggy is writing copy and condescendingly offers to review it
Peggy accepts Pete's offer to review her work

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of masculine performance — Don's calculated revenge, Pete's rifle fantasy, Betty's violent outburst as masculine aggression, Peggy navigating male condescension
Pete and Peggy's storylines intersect when he offers to review her copywriting work

events.event: Pete and Peggy arrive early at Sterling Cooper and have sex on his office sofa
Pete complains to Peggy that Trudy doesn't understand him
Pete confesses he never read Peggy's Belle Jolie copy, relieving her anxiety
Trudy arrives unannounced with champagne to celebrate their new apartment
Pete argues with Trudy for showing up without warning and sends her away
Bert Cooper gives Don a $2,500 bonus check for outstanding creative work
Cooper presses a copy of Atlas Shrugged on Don, urging him to read it
Sal has dinner with Belle Jolie executive Elliott and they connect deeply
Elliott invites Sal to his hotel room but Sal declines out of fear
Don goes to Midge's apartment intending to surprise her with a Paris trip
Don finds Midge with her beatnik friends preparing to smoke marijuana
Don joins them smoking marijuana and takes a Polaroid of Midge and Roy
Don realizes from the photo that Midge and Roy are in love
Midge turns down Don's Paris trip offer
Don signs over his $2,500 bonus check to Midge for a car
Young Dick meets a hobo who asks for food in exchange for work
Dick's stepmother Abigail invites the hobo to stay despite Archie's refusal
The hobo tells young Dick about giving up family and responsibilities for freedom
The hobo shows Dick the hobo code symbols for communicating about houses
Archie refuses to pay the hobo as promised and the hobo leaves
Dick finds the hobo carved 'dishonest man lives here' on their fence post

interactions.description: all storylines explore dishonesty — Don's false identity, his affair with Midge, Pete's betrayal of Trudy
the hobo flashback reframes Don's present behavior through his childhood lesson about dishonesty

events.event: Jim Hobart offers Don a position at McCann Erickson with over 500 people at his disposal
Hobart tells Betty she resembles Grace Kelly and offers her a Coca-Cola modeling job
Betty tells Don she wants to take the modeling job
Don expresses concern about Betty working but eventually relents
Betty goes to the Coca-Cola photo shoot and enjoys rediscovering her modeling identity
The family dog Polly kills one of the neighbor's pigeons
Neighbor Ross threatens to shoot the dog if it kills another pigeon
Pete devises a strategy to block Kennedy ads by buying swing state space for Secor Laxatives
Cooper and Roger praise Pete for his Nixon campaign initiative
Peggy's skirt rips at the office due to weight gain
Joan lends Peggy a replacement outfit and advises her to lose weight
Peggy tells Joan she's more interested in becoming a writer than attracting men
Joan dismisses Peggy's writing ambitions as a ploy to get closer to Paul Kinsey
Don takes Betty's photos to Roger's office and negotiates a raise to $45,000
Don promises Roger he will never leave Sterling Cooper for another advertising agency
McCann drops Betty from the campaign, claiming they wanted an Audrey Hepburn type
Betty lies to Don, claiming she quit the modeling job to stay home
Betty walks into the yard in a negligee and shoots at the neighbor's pigeons with Bobby's BB gun

interactions.description: all storylines explore characters maintaining false fronts - Don hiding his identity, Betty pretending contentment, their marriage built on deception, Peggy hiding her ambitions behind feminine expectations
McCann's recruitment ploy brings Don's professional identity crisis into direct conflict with Betty's personal awakening and their marital dynamics
the audience knows Don's entire identity is false, while Betty believes she's the one living a lie by wanting to work

events.event: Betty's father Gene visits with his new companion Gloria, whom Betty openly dislikes
Betty, Gene, and the children leave for a weekend trip without Don
Pete informs Don that Dr. Scholl's is leaving the agency due to creative dissatisfaction
The office watches Kennedy's light campaign ad versus Nixon's dull one
Roger invites identical twin models Eleanor and her sister for drinks after hours
Roger flirts aggressively with Eleanor, commenting on her translucent skin
Don remains detached and eventually leaves the twins encounter
Roger has sex with Eleanor a second time and suffers a massive heart attack
Roger is rushed to the hospital where his wife Mona and daughter Margaret visit
Roger breaks down crying and tells Mona how much he loves her
Carol confesses to Joan that she is in love with her and wants to be with her
Joan listens impassively and deflects Carol's confession without cruelty
Joan is called to the office to handle the crisis of Roger's heart attack
Joan works through the night with Bert Cooper drafting telegrams to clients
Cooper tells Joan she is capable of more than she gives herself credit for
Don calls Betty but does not reach her after Roger's heart attack
Don goes to Rachel's apartment seeking connection
Rachel resists at first but tells Don to continue when he hesitates
Don and Rachel sleep together
Don reveals his traumatic past to Rachel: prostitute mother, drunk father, cruel household
Joan composes the last telegrams in the early morning, holding back tears

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of intimacy and the vulnerability it requires — Don's emotional honesty with Rachel, Roger's physical excess leading to breakdown, Betty's distance from family
Rachel receives Don's most honest confession but still doesn't know his real name is Dick Whitman
Don's affair with Rachel directly conflicts with his marriage to Betty, coming to a head when he seeks Rachel instead of his wife

events.event: Adam Whitman mails a package to Don Draper before hanging himself in his hotel room
Roger Sterling collapses with a second heart attack during the Lucky Strike meeting
Mona Sterling berates Cooper and the client for endangering Roger's health
Cooper offers Don a partnership with Roger's office and senior role
Don accepts the partnership offer
Peggy tests the Relaxacizor and discovers its pleasurable effects
Peggy reframes the campaign as 'The Rejuvenator' focused on making women feel good
Peggy presents her Rejuvenator concept to Don who recognizes its potential
Peggy asks Don for a fifteen percent raise and her own desk
Don tells Peggy to demand what she wants rather than ask politely, and grants her request
Peggy is dismissive and rude to her gentleman date arranged by her mother
Pete signs for and opens Adam's package containing Don's past identity documents
Betty is stirred by the air conditioning salesman's visit and nearly invites him upstairs
Don tells Betty he made partner and she confesses about letting the salesman inside
Betty fantasizes about the salesman while leaning against the vibrating washing machine

episodes.theme: the pursuit of satisfaction and its consequences

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of desire and satisfaction — professional recognition, sexual fulfillment, and power
Pete now knows Don's secret identity while Don remains unaware of this threat
Adam's death and the package he sends directly threaten Don's false identity

events.event: Sterling Cooper employees gather for election night party with early Nixon odds at 22 to 1
Ken chases secretary Allison and pulls her to the ground, hiking up her skirt
Paul stages reading of his play with Sal as lead, Joan holding script
Sal pulls Joan in for exaggerated dramatic kiss, Joan senses something is off
Don chooses Duck Phillips for Head of Account Services, passing over Pete
Pete examines Adam's package containing photos labeled 'Dick and Adam, 1944' and military dog tags
Pete confronts Don with evidence that Dick Whitman died in Korea but Don is alive
Pete demands Head of Account Services job in exchange for silence about Don's identity
Dick Whitman and Lt. Draper are at forward position when gasoline ignites from cigarette
Dick finds Lt. Draper dead, switches dog tags and assumes his identity
Don refuses to give Pete the job despite blackmail threat
Don proposes to Rachel that they run away to Los Angeles together immediately
Rachel refuses Don's proposal, saying he just wants to run away, and ends the affair
Peggy tells Don about her frustration with doing right and being resented while wrongdoers face no consequences
Pete tells Bert Cooper everything about Don's stolen identity
Cooper dismisses Pete with 'who cares?' and tells Don a man is whatever room he's in
Cooper instructs Don to either fire Pete or find a way to keep his loyalty
Don sits alone watching Nixon's concession speech on television

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of transformation — Don's stolen identity, Rachel's refusal to enable his escape, Pete's failed power play, Peggy's moral frustration with workplace dynamics
Pete's discovery of Don's secret becomes leverage for his career ambitions, forcing both storylines to climax simultaneously
Rachel sees Don's proposal as escape rather than love, not knowing the specific threat driving his panic

events.event: Duck Phillips secures Kodak meeting and instructs accounts team to be aggressive
Don promotes Peggy to Junior Copywriter and assigns her Clearasil
Pete storms out furious that Peggy got credit for Clearasil account
Tom pressures Pete to focus on producing grandchildren instead of work
Harry sleeps in office after Jennifer kicked him out for cheating
Harry makes desperate calls to Jennifer begging to come home
Don tells Betty he can't come to Thanksgiving because of work
Francine arrives in tears about Carlton's affair, prompting Betty to examine her marriage
Betty checks phone bill and discovers Don has been calling her therapist
Betty tells Dr. Wayne therapy helped and suspects Don's infidelity
Peggy begins feeling severe abdominal pain she tries to hide
Joan moves Peggy to shared office and reminds her not to forget where she came from
Doctor tells Peggy she's expecting, she insists it's impossible
Peggy gives birth and refuses to look at the baby
Don reframes Kodak projector as emotional Carousel device
Don delivers devastating Carousel pitch using his own family photos
Harry weeps and leaves during Don's presentation
Kodak signs with Sterling Cooper
Don rushes home wanting to join family for Thanksgiving
Don finds empty house, Betty and children left without him

interactions.description: all storylines explore how maintaining false facades leads to isolation and loss
the audience knows Don's deceptions while Betty is just discovering them
Don's Carousel pitch about family love directly leads to his attempt to reconnect with Betty
